beak.utilities.file_io module

beak.utilities.file_io.__get_dtype_range_info(dtype: str | dtype) → Tuple[number, number][source]

Determine the range of values for the raster array.

Replaces the rasterio.dtypes.get_dtype_range_info() since this function returns “float64” type for min and max values of “float32”.

Parameters:

dtype – A dtype to determine the range for.

Returns:

The minimum and maximum values to the related data type.

Return type:

Tuple of np.number, np.number

Raises:

ValueError – If the dtype is not supported.

beak.utilities.file_io.__get_dtype_range_value(dtype: str | dtype) → int | float[source]

Get the range value by dtype.

Parameters:

dtype – The dtype to get the range value for.

Returns:

The range value for the given dtype.

Return type:

Union[int, float]

beak.utilities.file_io.__get_minimum_dtype(array: ndarray, unify_integer_types: bool = False) → str[source]

Determine the minimum data type for the raster array.

Comparison based on the raster’s minimum and maximum values. Alternative to rasterio.dtypes._get_minimum_dtype() since it does not return “int8”. According to the restrictions, smallest bit-size for outputs is: - 8 for integers - 32 for floats

Restrictions: - dtypes smaller than “uint8” are not supported by numpy and rasterio - dtype “float16” is not yet supported rasterio

Parameters:

  • array – The raster array to determine the minimum data type for.

  • unify_integer_types – Whether to differentiate signed and unsigned integers.

Returns:

The minimum data type for the raster array.

Return type:

str

Raises:

ValueError – If the dtype is not supported.

beak.utilities.file_io.__get_next_higher_dtype(dtype: str | dtype) → dtype[source]

Get the next higher dtype.

Parameters:

dtype – The dtype to get the next higher one for.

Returns:

The next higher dtype. If input dtype is not supported, return the original dtype.

Return type:

np.dtype

beak.utilities.file_io._cast_array_to_minimum_dtype(array: ndarray, value: int | float | None = None, unify_integer_types: bool = False) → Tuple[ndarray, int | float | None][source]

Determine the minimum data type for the raster array.

Parameters:

  • array – The raster array to determine the minimum data type for.

  • value – The value for additional comparison.

Returns:

The raster array and its metadata.

  • np.ndarray: The raster array with the minimum data type determined.

  • Dict: The updated metadata dictionary with the minimum data type set.

Return type:

Tuple of np.ndarray, Dict

beak.utilities.file_io._cast_value_to_int(value: int | float) → int | float[source]

Cast a value to integer type if possible.

Maximum to cast are “uint64” and “int64” types, which both return True when passed through np.can_cast().

Parameters:

value – The value to cast to integer type.

Returns:

The value cast to integer type if possible, otherwise the original value.

Return type:

Union[int, float]

beak.utilities.file_io._initialize_data_for_rasterization(array: ndarray, nodata_value: int | float | None, fill_value: int | float | None) → Tuple[ndarray, int | float, int | float][source]

Initialize data for rasterization by adjusting the array data type and handling nodata and fill values.

If the nodata value is None, nodata value will be initialized by the array’s and fill values. The array will be casted to the minimum possible dtype and nodata will be updated to an edge value: - for unsigned integers: positive boundary - for signed integers: negative boundary - for floating point numbers: negative boundary

If the nodata values is not None, the array will be casted to the minimum possible type,

without changing the nodata value.

Parameters:

  • array – Input data array.

  • nodata_value – Value representing empty pixels.

  • fill_value – Value used to fill areas.

Returns:

The processed array, nodata value, and fill value.

beak.utilities.file_io._remove_nan_rows(data: ndarray, axis: int = 1)[source]

Remove NaN values from the given data along the specified axis.

Parameters:

  • data – The array to be processed.

  • axis – The axis along which to remove NaN values. Defaults to 1.

Returns:

The array with NaN values removed.

beak.utilities.file_io._update_nodata(array: ndarray, nodata_value: int | float)[source]

Update nodata value based on the array’s values.

Sets the nodata value to a boundary value of the array if the provided nodata value is within the array’s range. Does not update anything else than this value.

Parameters:

  • array – The input array to be adjusted.

  • nodata_value – The nodata value.

Returns:

The updated nodata value.

beak.utilities.file_io.download_file_from_cdr(download_url: str) → BytesIO[source]

Download a raster file from the specified URL.

This function attempts to download a raster file from the given URL. If the download fails due to SSL verification issues, it retries the download without SSL verification.

Parameters:

download_url – The URL from which to download the raster file.

Returns:

A BytesIO object containing that can be used as path-like input.

Return type:

io.BytesIO

beak.utilities.file_io.load_layer(input_file: str) → Tuple[ndarray, Dict][source]

Load a raster file from the given file path.

Reshapes the output array to (-1, 1).

Parameters:

input_file – The file path to the raster file.

Returns:

The raster array and its metadata.

  • A reshaped numpy array.

  • The metadata of the raster.

beak.utilities.file_io.load_layers(input_files: List[str]) → ndarray[source]

Load a list of raster files from the given file paths.

Single rasters will be reshaped to (-1, 1) and stacked column-wise.

Parameters:

input_files – The list of file paths to the raster files.

Returns:

The stacked raster array with the data from all input files.

beak.utilities.file_io.prepare_model_data(input_layers: ndarray, input_labels: ndarray) → Tuple[ndarray, ndarray][source]

Remove NaN values from both layer and labels for training a model.

Parameters:

  • input_layers – The input raster layers.

  • input_labels – The input labels for the training data.

Returns:

The prepared model data with layers and labels

  • A numpy array containing the prepared training data.

  • A numpy array containing the labels.

beak.utilities.file_io.prepare_output(src_array: ndarray, src_meta: Dict) → Tuple[ndarray, Dict][source]

Prepare output for saving as a raster.

Builds a chain for

  • Changing NaN to the respective nodata value.

  • Casting to the minimum data type possible.

  • Update metadata.

Returns:

The prepared output raster array and its metadata.

  • A numpy array containing the prepared output.

  • The updated metadata.

beak.utilities.file_io.read_raster_band(raster: DatasetReader, band: int = 1, nodata_to_nan: bool = True, **kwargs) → Tuple[ndarray, Dict][source]

Read a raster file from the given DatasetReader object and returns the raster array and its metadata.

In preparation for iterating over multiple bands, the kwargs argument can take every valid key-value pair that is present in the input raster’s metadata dictionary. E.g., if different bands have different nodata values or data types, the metadata will be updated for further processing.

Parameters:

  • raster – The DatasetReader object from which to read the raster.

  • band – The band number to read from the raster.

  • nodata_to_nan – Whether to apply a mask to the raster array. Default is True.

  • **kwargs – Additional keyword arguments to update the metadata of the raster.

Returns:

The raster array and its updated metadata.

  • A 2-dimensional raster array read from the input raster.

  • The (updated) metadata of the raster read from the input raster.

beak.utilities.file_io.save_raster(src_array: ndarray, src_meta: Dict, output_path: str, compatibility_mode: bool = False, **kwargs)[source]

Save a raster array to a file with the specified metadata or profile.

Writes a given raster array to a file at the specified output path, using the provided metadata. It ensures that the minimum data type for the raster array is set and updates the metadata with any additional keyword arguments provided. The metadata can be replaced by the raster’s profile, providing additional parameters for tiling and block size in order to optimize storage and processing for COG compatibility.

Nodata value in the kwargs argument will overwrite the automatically determined nodata value.

Parameters:

  • src_array – The raster array to be saved.

  • src_meta – The metadata dictionary for the raster.

  • output_path – The file path where the raster will be saved.

  • compatibility_mode – A boolean indicating whether to enable QGIS compatibility for 8bit signed integers. the profile will be updated to support Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF (COG).

  • **kwargs – Additional keyword arguments to update the metadata of the raster.

Returns:

None

beak.utilities.file_io.write_json(folder_name: str, file_name: str, data: Dict)[source]

Write a json file.

Parameters:

  • folder_name – The folder name where the json file will be saved.

  • file_name – The name of the json file.

  • data – The data to be written in the json file.

Returns:

None
